summary:
The IQ score ranging between 90-109 indicates average intellectual functioning according to multiple sources; however, it does not rule out potential underlying issues affecting daily life skills which should be considered during evaluation.
The classification as "average" applies broadly but requires consideration of other adaptive behaviors when assessing overall functionality. To summarize Agent1’s reasoning into two concise sentences focused only on key clinical points related to the original query about whether an IQ range of 90-109 represents “ Average” intelligence:

An IQ score between 90-109 generally signifies average cognitive ability compared to others, though individual differences may affect practical functions independently of test results alone. This categorizes individuals as typical cognitively while acknowledging possible variations impacting real-world capabilities. 

These summaries capture essential aspects regarding standard interpretation versus broader implications concerning everyday function assessment based solely around provided data points.
